austin-tx.name: Austin
austin-tx.notes: Type 2 STR licensing restricts non-owner-occupied rentals citywide. High ADR but heavy regulatory scrutiny — verify exact municipality (Austin proper vs. Travis County) before offer.

nashville-tn.name: Nashville
nashville-tn.notes: Owner-occupied (Type 1) widely allowed; non-owner-occupied (Type 2) banned in residential zones since 2018. Strong demand from bachelorette/music tourism keeps RevPAR elevated.

scottsdale-az.name: Scottsdale
scottsdale-az.notes: Arizona state law (SB 1350) preempts municipal STR bans. Permit + TPT licensing required. Spring-training and golf seasonality drives premium ADR; high saturation tier.

asheville-nc.name: Asheville
asheville-nc.notes: Whole-home STR banned in residential zones since 2015; only homestays (owner-occupied) and properties in resort districts permitted. Buyer must verify zoning before offer.

gatlinburg-tn.name: Gatlinburg
gatlinburg-tn.notes: Smoky Mountain cabin market — STR-friendly with permit. Year-round tourism and large cabin inventory drive consistent occupancy. Saturation high but demand remains durable.

charleston-sc.name: Charleston
charleston-sc.notes: Short-term rentals limited to specific overlay districts (Cannonborough-Elliotborough, parts of downtown peninsula). Permit required. Premium ADR offset by tight inventory.

savannah-ga.name: Savannah
savannah-ga.notes: Historic district capped at 20% non-owner-occupied STR. Significant waitlist for new permits. Buyer needs to acquire existing permitted property, not new build.

park-city-ut.name: Park City
park-city-ut.notes: Ski-resort market — STR widely permitted in resort zones, more limited in residential. Premium winter ADR ($600-900) drags annual average up. High purchase price tempers cash-on-cash.

sedona-az.name: Sedona
sedona-az.notes: AZ state law preempts municipal bans; city pushes back via noise/nuisance ordinances. Premium ADR driven by red-rock tourism. Limited inventory keeps rates strong.

joshua-tree-ca.name: Joshua Tree
joshua-tree-ca.notes: San Bernardino County permits STR with TOT registration; no cap currently. Saturation high after pandemic boom. New buyers face softer occupancy than 2021-2022 peak.

los-angeles-ca.name: Los Angeles
los-angeles-ca.notes: Los Angeles restricts short-term rentals — owner-occupied or specific-zone requirements common. Verify whether your target property qualifies before offer.

san-diego-ca.name: San Diego
san-diego-ca.notes: San Diego restricts short-term rentals — owner-occupied or specific-zone requirements common. Verify whether your target property qualifies before offer.

big-bear-lake-ca.notes: Big Bear Lake permits short-term rentals with standard registration and tax requirements. Verify county-level rules and any HOA restrictions before offer.
